name: Rotating Rostering - CR1
content: Night shifts are considered more demanding than other shifts. The roster must now ensure that no employee works more than 2 night shifts in a row, while the existing minimum and maximum repetition rules for the other shift types remain unchanged.
value_info: `n_days_per_week`: Integer number of days in a week (always 7).
`n_weeks`: Integer number of weeks in the roster cycle.
`s_min`: Integer minimum run length for any shift type.
`s_max`: Integer maximum run length for any shift type (applies to non-night shifts).
`requirements`: List[List[int]] of shape [n_days_per_week][4], where requirements[d][s] is the number of employees required on weekday d for shift s (0=off, 1=early, 2=late, 3=night).

var1.descr: `x_roster`: List[int] of length n_days (= n_weeks * n_days_per_week), where x_roster[d] is the shift assigned on flattened day d of the cyclic roster (0=off, 1=early, 2=late, 3=night).

var2.descr: `y_roster`: List[List[int]] of shape [n_weeks][n_days_per_week], where y_roster[w][d] is the shift assigned on day d of week w.

complexity.difficulty_rationale: Adds a single localized constraint family that caps consecutive night shifts at 2, without touching any other variable or constraint group.

cr_impact.notes: Adds one constraint family (night_max_run) over all 56 cyclic day positions in the x roster variable.
